title: Service Activation and Billing Mediation Latency
spl:
index=telecom sourcetype="mediation:event"
| bin _time span=5m
| stats avg(latency_ms) as avg_lat, perc95(latency_ms) as p95_lat, max(queue_depth) as max_q, sum(records_in) as vol by _time
| where avg_lat > 120000 OR p95_lat > 300000 OR max_q > 500000
| eval avg_min=round(avg_lat/60000,2), p95_min=round(p95_lat/60000,2)
| table _time, avg_min, p95_min, max_q, vol
description: Mediation pipelines must deliver rated records to billing within windows; end-to-end latency and backlog depth prevent revenue leakage and rating disputes distinct from raw CDR analytics use cases.
value: Service and risk leaders use signals from Service Activation and Billing Mediation Latency to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.
implementation: Normalize timestamps to when batches complete, not file arrival. Alert on sustained queue growth with derivative search on `queue_depth`. Coordinate thresholds with billing close calendar. **Domain context:** Mediation/rating sits between network CDRs and billing‚Äîlatency spikes near invoice close or rate plan changes are common; separate batch vs streaming pipelines in tagging. **Splunk:** Thresholds `120000`/`300000` ms are examples‚Äîtie to billing window SLAs; `avg_lat`/`p95_lat` on 5m buckets can be noisy for hourly batches.
visualization: Time chart (latency and queue depth), Area chart (records processed), Single value (backlog breach).
knownFalsePositives: Latency noise during mediation batching, tax or rating rule pushes, Diameter peer certificate rolls, or billing cycle catch-up the BSS operations desk owns.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Mediation platform logs via HEC.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=telecom` `sourcetype="mediation:event"` (batch_id, records_in, records_out, latency_ms, queue_depth).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Normalize timestamps to when batches complete, not file arrival. Alert on sustained queue growth with derivative search on `queue_depth`. Coordinate thresholds with billing close calendar. **Domain context:** Mediation/rating sits between network CDRs and billing‚Äîlatency spikes near invoice close or rate plan changes are common; separate batch vs streaming pipelines in tagging. **Splunk:** Thresholds `120000`/`300000` ms are examples‚Äîtie to billing window SLAs; `avg_lat`/`p95_lat` on 5m buckets ‚Ä¶

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=telecom sourcetype="mediation:event"
| bin _time span=5m
| stats avg(latency_ms) as avg_lat, perc95(latency_ms) as p95_lat, max(queue_depth) as max_q, sum(records_in) as vol by _time
| where avg_lat > 120000 OR p95_lat > 300000 OR max_q > 500000
| eval avg_min=round(avg_lat/60000,2), p95_min=round(p95_lat/60000,2)
| table _time, avg_min, p95_min, max_q, vol
```

Understanding this SPL

**Service Activation and Billing Mediation Latency** ‚Äî Mediation pipelines must deliver rated records to billing within windows; end-to-end latency and backlog depth prevent revenue leakage and rating disputes distinct from raw CDR analytics use cases.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=telecom` `sourcetype="mediation:event"` (batch_id, records_in, records_out, latency_ms, queue_depth). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Mediation platform logs via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: telecom; **sourcetype**: mediation:event. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=telecom, sourcetype="mediation:event". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where avg_lat > 120000 OR p95_lat > 300000 OR max_q > 500000` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **avg_min** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Service Activation and Billing Mediation Latency**): table _time, avg_min, p95_min, max_q, vol


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare KPIs or record counts with the EMS/performance management export or mediation control room view for the same element and interval. Align vendor time zones and collection periods before tuning thresholds.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart (latency and queue depth), Area chart (records processed), Single value (backlog breach).
grandmaExplanation: We watch service activation and billing delays. We help you catch handoff breaks before revenue and NPS take a hit.
